violations.text: Yogurts and Containers of cut fruit/melon in the customer reach-in cooler #2 was observed at 44-50 degrees Fahrenheit. Food items observed at 44 degrees or less were moved to colder refrigeration. Food items observed at 45 degrees and higher were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026

The customer reach-in cooler #2 was observed to have excessive ice buildup and not maintaining an ambient temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below as required for Time/Temperature Control for Safety (TCS) Foods. Food items were removed and unit was taken out of service at time of inspection. Use of unit for TCS Foods is to be discontinued until serviced/repaired and properly maintaining 41 degrees Fahrenheit or less. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026
